claim.content: Given a discrete set of DMC-computed occupied-band energy values $\{\mathcal{E}(k_j)\}$ at reciprocal-space grid points $k_j$ available in a finite periodic simulation cell, the occupied-band shape on the interval $0\le k\le k_F$ is represented by fitting a quartic polynomial $\mathcal{E}(k)=\alpha_0+\alpha_2 k^2+\alpha_4 k^4$ by least squares to the DMC points and using the analytic derivative $\partial\mathcal{E}/\partial k = 2\alpha_2 k + 4\alpha_4 k^3$ evaluated at $k=k_F$ to obtain the quasiparticle effective mass $m^{*}=k_F / (\partial\mathcal{E}/\partial k)_{k_F}$; this procedure assumes that a three-parameter quartic model is sufficiently flexible to capture the true band curvature over the fitting range so that the derivative at $k_F$ is reliable and not significantly biased by higher-order $k$-dependence or by fitting over points far from $k_F$.
